# UC-16.3.14 · Priority 1–2 First-Assignment Latency

## Description

Time-to-first-assignment for urgent incidents measures triage discipline and major-incident readiness; reducing latency supports ITIL event-to-incident handling and response SLA guardrails.

## Value

Time-to-first-assignment for urgent incidents measures triage discipline and major-incident readiness; reducing latency supports ITIL event-to-incident handling and response SLA guardrails.

## Implementation

(1) Confirm which timestamp represents first assignment in your instance; (2) Exclude bot-created tickets with `caller_id` filters if needed; (3) Alert when `p90_lag_mins` exceeds the response playbook target for two rolling weeks for any group supporting P1/P2.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_snow`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`priority`, `opened_at`, `assigned_at`, `assignment_group`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Confirm which timestamp represents first assignment in your instance; (2) Exclude bot-created tickets with `caller_id` filters if needed; (3) Alert when `p90_lag_mins` exceeds the response playbook target for two rolling weeks for any group supporting P1/P2.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d priority IN (1,2)
| eval open_ts=coalesce(opened_at, strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval assign_ts=coalesce(assigned_at, first_response_time)
| where isnotnull(assign_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts)
| eval assign_lag_mins=round((assign_ts-open_ts)/60,1)
| where assign_lag_mins>=0 AND assign_lag_mins<10080
| stats median(assign_lag_mins) as med_lag_mins, perc90(assign_lag_mins) as p90_lag_mins by assignment_group
| sort -p90_lag_mins
```

Understanding this SPL

**Priority 1–2 First-Assignment Latency** — Time-to-first-assignment for urgent incidents measures triage discipline and major-incident readiness; reducing latency supports ITIL event-to-incident handling and response SLA guardrails.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`priority`, `opened_at`, `assigned_at`, `assignment_group`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: itsm; **sourcetype**: snow:incident. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=itsm, sourcetype="snow:incident", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **open_ts** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **assign_ts** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where isnotnull(assign_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **assign_lag_mins** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where assign_lag_mins>=0 AND assign_lag_mins<10080` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by assignment_group** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management by All_Ticket_Management.status, All_Ticket_Management.priority, All_Ticket_Management.category | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Priority 1–2 First-Assignment Latency** — Time-to-first-assignment for urgent incidents measures triage discipline and major-incident readiness; reducing latency supports ITIL event-to-incident handling and response SLA guardrails.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=itsm` `sourcetype="snow:incident"` (`priority`, `opened_at`, `assigned_at`, `assignment_group`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_snow`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Ticket_Management.All_Ticket_Management` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ServiceNow list or form that matches this data (for example the incident, request, or problem view). Reconcile a few record numbers, states, and times. Confirm the add-on poll or export time lines up with what you see in the browser.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Bar chart (p90 first-assignment minutes by group), Table (group medians), Line chart (weekly p90 trend).

## SPL

```spl
index=itsm sourcetype="snow:incident" earliest=-30d priority IN (1,2)
| eval open_ts=coalesce(opened_at, strptime(opened_at,"%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"))
| eval assign_ts=coalesce(assigned_at, first_response_time)
| where isnotnull(assign_ts) AND isnotnull(open_ts)
| eval assign_lag_mins=round((assign_ts-open_ts)/60,1)
| where assign_lag_mins>=0 AND assign_lag_mins<10080
| stats median(assign_lag_mins) as med_lag_mins, perc90(assign_lag_mins) as p90_lag_mins by assignment_group
| sort -p90_lag_mins
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (p90 first-assignment minutes by group), Table (group medians), Line chart (weekly p90 trend).
